qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Deeply nested XML causes stack exhaustion in DOMParser endDocument normalization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is about verbatim serialization of DocumentType publicId/systemId/internalSubset fields in the DOCTYPE branch of XMLSerializer after createDocumentType. This finding is a parse-time recursion bug in DOMParser -> Document.normalize that causes stack exhaustion and does not involve DocumentType serialization.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unchecked element names permit XML element injection during serialization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets unchecked element tagName handling in Document.createElement and the normal element serialization path. GHSA-f6ww-3ggp-fr8h instead targets unvalidated DocumentType fields emitted in the serializer's DOCTYPE branch via createDocumentType or direct DocumentType property writes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause in DocumentType serialization. The retained qualifying findings cover a recursive post-parse normalization DoS and unchecked element-name serialization, both distinct from the advisory's createDocumentType/DOCTYPE emission flaw.
evidence: GHSA-f6ww-3ggp-fr8h describes XML injection caused by verbatim emission of DocumentType internalSubset, publicId, and systemId in XMLSerializer after createDocumentType stores those fields without validation.
deeply_nested_xml_parse_dos reports DOMParser.parseFromString reaching Document.normalize recursion and RangeError stack exhaustion, which is a different entrypoint, code path, and impact class.

element_name_output_injection reports createElement(tagName) plus verbatim element tag serialization, which is a separate serializer branch from the advisory's DOCTYPE handling and does not rely on DocumentType fields.
